negativeEvidence.strongNegativeEvidence: Cook County Health hub EXPLICITLY does not offer shadowing or observership. The only visiting-medical-student lane referenced is via affiliated school program coordinators — meaning a student's home school must already have an affiliation arrangement with Cook County Health. Not an open public pathway for self-applying IMGs.
negativeEvidence.weakNegativeEvidence: The 2018 International Medical Student Packet PDF is 8 years old; verbatim retrieval of current 2026 international-specialty (Trauma/Anesthesia/Burn) policy was not achieved this turn. Queued for manual retry.

nextAction: Manual retry queued: (a) extract verbatim audience+specialty paragraph from current International-Medical-Student-Packet PDF (need scripts/p101-extract-pdf-text.ts on the URL); (b) confirm whether the 2018 PDF is still current policy. Until verified, classify as POSSIBLE_USCE_NEEDS_REVIEW — observership is verbatim NOT offered, US visiting-student lane is AFFILIATED-SCHOOL-COORDINATOR mediated only, international 3-specialty lane is unconfirmed.
plainEnglishSummary: Cook County Health (which runs Stroger Hospital) does NOT offer shadowing or observership — that's verbatim on their education hub. They do allow fourth-year US medical students to do clinical rotations, but only through affiliated school program coordinators, not by direct student self-application. A 2018 PDF mentions international medical students may be accepted in 3 specialties (Trauma, Anesthesia, Burn) but the current 2026 policy was not directly verified this turn. Conservative classification: needs review before any public-facing claim.
driftCheck: This advanced national USCE discovery because Cook County Stroger was correctly classified as POSSIBLE_USCE_NEEDS_REVIEW rather than overclaimed as an open IMG-friendly Chicago safety-net — the verbatim 'no observership/shadowing' protects against the most common misclassification of this institution.

userFacingSummaryDraft.oneSentenceSummary: Cook County Health (Stroger Hospital) explicitly does NOT offer shadowing or observership; clinical rotations available only to fourth-year US MS via affiliated school program coordinator.
userFacingSummaryDraft.whoThisIsFor: Fourth-year US medical students whose home school has an affiliation arrangement with Cook County Health.
userFacingSummaryDraft.whoThisIsNotFor: Anyone seeking shadowing or observership (explicitly excluded verbatim); IMG graduates; self-applying students without an affiliated school coordinator.
userFacingSummaryDraft.howToApply: Through the student's home medical school's program coordinator — Cook County does not accept direct student applications for visiting status.
keyCaveats: CRITICAL: Observership and shadowing explicitly NOT offered (verbatim)
Clinical rotations only via affiliated school program coordinator
2018 International Medical Student Packet PDF mentions 3 specialties (Trauma/Anesthesia/Burn) for international — UNVERIFIED for 2026
Safety-net public hospital — overclaim risk is high; conservative classification
Stroger Hospital named as teaching site
userFacingSummaryDraft.whyWeClassifiedItThisWay: POSSIBLE_USCE_NEEDS_REVIEW because the hub explicitly excludes observership and shadowing, and the only clinical lane is gated by home-school affiliation — not a public self-application pathway. 2018 international PDF unverified.
userFacingSummaryDraft.sourceTransparencyNote: Verified at https://cookcountyhealth.org/education-and-research/ on 2026-05-11; 2018 PDF packet queued for retry
